body:
A single-fix patch release that corrects the standalone installer's zstd archive selection logic on systems where the `zstd` binary is not installed.

## Fixed

- **Standalone installer no longer selects zstd archives when `zstd` is not installed** -- The `tar_supports_zstd()` function returned `true` for GNU tar >= 1.31 regardless of whether the `zstd` binary was actually present on the system. Since GNU tar shells out to `zstd` rather than linking against it, this caused extraction to fail. The fix checks for the `zstd` binary upfront before evaluating the tar version. A separate fallback branch in `get_ext()` that could also select a `.tar.zst` archive without verifying zstd availability has been removed. [#8460](https://github.com/jdx/mise/pull/8460) by @octo

## New Contributors

* @octo made their first contribution in [#8460](https://github.com/jdx/mise/pull/8460)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jdx/mise/compare/v2026.3.2...v2026.3.3
name: v2026.3.3: Standalone installer zstd fix
